observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual Physical count of PPE, include all the PPE in the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the report to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and
b. Refer to COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of the next one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; this circular is issued for a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; and in no case shall the herein procedures be used to further derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs and/or cleanse subsequent discrepancies or unreconciled balances in PPE accounts. (CY 2022 AAR Observation No. 1; CYs 2016 to 2021 AAR & CY 2013 AAR Observation No. 2)
observations.reason: Property Cards were not updated and fully reconciled with the PPE accounts recorded in the books. Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual Physical count of PPE, include all the PPE in the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the report to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Officer-in-Charge to reconcile their balances to present fairly the PPE balance in the Financial Statements.
Reiterated in this report.
observations.reason: Physical count of all PPE was not conducted for CY 2024. The GSO submitted the RPCPPE and the PPEs purchased in CY 2024 only; No reconciliation between the Municipal Accountant and GSO records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Coordinate and obtain necessary documents to support valid reconciling items from the prior and current years and make appropriate adjustments in the BRS and other financial reports to fairly present the balances of Cash in Bank Accounts in the Financial Statements; (Reiterated in this report.)
b. Summarize all reconciling items without available supporting documents and references, and submit the same with a query to the COA Government Accountancy Sector (GAS) for its appropriate accounting treatment and remedies to clean the LGU’s books.
Reiterated in this report.
observations.reason: The recommendation was not fully complied by Management. The Management is still on the process of verifying and reconciling the data. The Audit Team has not received a copy of their query to COA GAS as to the appropriate accounting treatment of the reconciling items without available supporting documents. Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of stale checks particularly those issued in the prior years. Replacement checks maybe issued upon verifying the stale check;
b. Practice sending written notices to payees at least one month before the checks become stale; and
c. Submit to the Municipal Accountant all supporting documents related to the stale checks to serve as the basis for making adjusting entries. Moreover, prepare adjusting entries for all verified stale checks in order to properly present the cash in bank account and recognize the appropriate liability accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The recommendation was not fully complied by Management. The Management is still on the process of retrieval of prior years’ records. Replacement checks maybe issued upon submission of the verified stale check; Retrieval of documents is still on-going. Several DVs submitted lack the necessary supporting documents.
Reiterated in this report. Various projects are still on-going implementation as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation:

c) After accurately establishing the unutilized balances, return them to the source agencies or the National Treasury.
observations.reason: Retrieval of documents is still on-going. Management has difficulty in retrieving prior years’ records. Records of the Municipal Accountant not yet reconciled with the records of the Iloilo Provincial Government. Retrieval of documents is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct the physical count
of all PPEs to establish the existence, validity and accuracy of PPEs and
ensure timely and complete submission of the RPCPPE to the Office of
the Auditor;
b. Municipal Accountant and GSO to prepare their respective reports and
work closely in the reconciliation and updating of their records; and

1.14 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor avail of the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020, to finally establish PPE balances that are
verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

2.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that Official Receipts are issued from the proper Fund depending
on the nature of collections.
b. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct the
recorded relevant account balances in the Municipality’s books of
accounts as of December 31, 2024;
c. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze the
transactions and record the same to appropriate accounts to ensure the
fairness of presentation of the financial statements.

3.14 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require the Accountable Officers to liquidate immediately their
outstanding cash advances to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash
advances and ensure that succeeding cash advances are in conformance
to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 to attain fair presentation
of the relevant accounts in the financial statements;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting cash
advances to officials and employees unless previous cash advances are
properly accounted for or settled; and
c. Through the Municipal Accountant, send demand letters to require the
former accountable officials and employees who already separated from
service to liquidate or refund immediately the long overdue cash
advances granted to them.

4.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to continuously
coordinate and obtain necessary documents to support reconciling
items identified in the BRS;
b. Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of adjusting entries of
reconciling items with available documentation or reference to present
the correct balance of the Cash in Bank in the books of accounts; and
c. Municipal Accountant to summarize all reconciling items without
available supporting documents and references, and submit the same
with a query to the COA Government Accountancy Sector (GAS) for its
appropriate accounting treatment and remedies to clean the LGU’s
books.

5.19 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory count
at least twice a year to establish the actual stock on hand as of the end
of a given period and to determine whether there were any losses or
spoilage in order to fairly present the inventory accounts in the
financial statements. Moreover, submit to the Office of the Auditor the
Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July
31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively; and
b. Direct the concerned offices to strictly adhere to the prescribed
procedures on the requisition, issuance, and recording of
supplies/materials, in accordance with government accounting and
supply management guidelines.

5.20 Also, we recommended and Management agreed to establish a proper
inventory management system to ensure adequate documentation and
accurate recording of supplies/inventories in the books and to avoid future
misstatements in the financial records.

6.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Accountant agreed to send demand letters to require the accountable
officers concerned to immediately liquidate or refund the overdue cash
advances related to the petty cash, otherwise initiate the enforcement of
necessary sanctions for its non-settlement such as withholding of salary of
or non-issuance of agency clearance to former Petty Cash Custodians.

7.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to ensure the timely submission of the certified
Summary of RPT Collectibles showing the accurate value of
receivables to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year
for the setting up of receivables;
b. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the RPT and SET
receivables at the beginning of the year based solely on the certified
list of due and collectible real property taxes as submitted by the
Municipal Treasurer; and
c. Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to the amount
recognized as Receivables and Deferred Income to exclude fines and
penalties.

8.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Examine the nature of the variance and make the necessary
adjustments, subject to proper documentation; and
b. Coordinate with the concerned suppliers/creditors to verify
outstanding balances and maintain updated records to facilitate
payment of valid claims.

8.12 Also, we recommended and Management agreed to enhance internal
controls by regularly preforming periodic review and account
reconciliations and ensuring timely and accurate recording of
obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal Accounting Office a copy of the report of completed projects with necessary supporting documents;
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Accountant to coordinate and reconcile their records to determine the updated status and correct balances of the CIP accounts; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the identified completed projects to the appropriate PPE accounts and to record the related accumulated depreciation and the corresponding depreciation expense.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Ensure that all checks released to claimants are reported in the Report of Checks issued;
b. Verify and monitor all receipts and check releases to ensure that all transactions are completely recorded in the appropriate time/period; (Delays in recording transactions persist due to the late return or submission of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) to the accounting office.)
c. Strengthen internal control on the release of checks and ensure that DVs are returned by the payees/suppliers to avoid delays in the recording of transactions; and
d. Submit the original disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: The recommendation was not fully complied by Management, as delays in recording transactions persist due to the late return or submission of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) to the accounting office. Several DVs submitted lack the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the M&E Team, composed of members as stated in Section 5.2 of PCW, DILG, NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee, through its Secretariat to:
a. Post the invitation or request for submission of price quotations/proposals in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity for a period of at least three (3) calendar days for procurement falling under those mentioned in Section 54.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No 9184; and
b. Post the notice of award, contract or purchase order, including notice to proceed, if necessary, in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity in all instances of alternative methods of procurement, except for contracts with ABC of Fifty Thousand Pesos (₱50,000.00) and below.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to appropriately record the purchases of relief goods and other supplies as inventory and recognized its related expense as required in COA Circular No. 2014-002 and IPSAS Nos. 1 and 12 in order to fairly present the inventory balances and related expense accounts presented in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Human Resource Management Officer and all other personnel concerned to:
a) Print the Municipal Employees' DTR; b) Track absences, tardiness, and undertime, and subtract from leave credits and c) Put in place the necessary safeguards, such as keeping a logbook, to address the unrecorded entries caused by mechanical error.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to hasten the operation of the Waste Processing System Machines. We also recommended the Management that development projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the PRAISE Committee to stop awarding monetary incentives if there are no monetary savings identified to have resulted from the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personnel efforts of employees-grantees. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the officials concerned to submit the supporting documents that show the computation of monetary savings, specific criteria, performance indicators and procedures established, and individual appraisal or evaluation reports assessing the performance and conduct for each employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit fully and promptly the inter-agency payables to the concerned government agencies in compliance with the above-mentioned laws and regulations. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the validity of the non-moving prior years’ balances of Due to BIR and Due to GSIS accounts and reconcile the amounts withheld/deducted against the amounts remitted and remit immediately the valid payables.
observations.reason: Already complied by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO- Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant, at least quarterly, to reconcile between the cashbooks and the accounting records be done so that Cash in Local Treasury is accurately presented in the financial statements and in the cashbooks.
observations.reason: An unreconciled amount continues to exist between the cashbook and the accounting records. The cashbooks maintained by the Municipal Treasurer have not been updated; Further follow-up is ongoing.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the general fund for the 20% Development Fund as required under Section 313 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 105 of the New Government Accounting System for LGU’s Volume I.

We recommend the submission of the above-mentioned documents to allow us to properly evaluate the transaction. Failure to submit the requested documents will constrain us to issue the appropriate Notice of Suspension requiring the submission of those documents. The said Notice of Suspension if not settled within 90 days from receipt will mature into an audit disallowance.

Furthermore, we recommend that the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), members of the BAC Secretariat and members of the Technical Working Group (TWG) should familiarize themselves with the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and it’s Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation and ensure that all procurements of the municipality should be in conformity with that law.
observations.managementAction: Documents were forwarded by previous Auditor to TSO for inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) members, members of the BAC Secretariat and members of the Technical Working Group (TWG) should familiarize themselves with the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and it’s Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation and ensure that all procurements of the municipality should be in conformity with that law.

We recommend that the OIC Municipal Treasurer deposit all undeposited collections to the authorized depository bank. Strictly adhere to the rule which requires the deposit of collections to the authorized depository bank not later than next banking day.

We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive require, Ms. Celma S. Barrera, former Municipal Treasurer to deposit her undeposited collections.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require Ms. Depamaylo and Mr. Saito to produce and remit the remaining balance of their unremitted collections.

We recommend that the municipality clarify/reconcile their records with the Department of Budget and Management, particularly SARO No. G-13-00856 in the amount of P1,200,000.00 since the fund was not received by the municipality.  We further recommend that the remaining balance of the DAP Fund be returned to the National Treasury pursuant to the Supreme Court declaration of it unconstitutionality.
